message:
feat(stdlib/strpct): port strpct/strspct percentage formatters (bd-cfslu)

Adds the NetBSD libutil percentage formatters used by df, iostat,
and similar tools to render packed `num / denom` ratios as
human-readable percentages.

New C entry points:

- `char *strpct(char *buf, size_t bufsize, uintmax_t num,
               uintmax_t denom, size_t precision);`
- `char *strspct(char *buf, size_t bufsize, intmax_t num,
                intmax_t denom, size_t precision);`

Each renders `100 * num / denom` rounded to `precision`
fractional digits into `buf` (NUL-terminated, snprintf-style
truncation), and returns `buf` on success or NULL when `buf` is
NULL or `bufsize` is zero. `denom == 0` writes `"0"` (or
`"0.000…0"`) per NetBSD's defensive convention. `strspct`
preserves the sign of `num XOR denom`; a true zero never carries
a `-` sign.

Implementation:

- core: pure-safe `format_percent_unsigned` / `format_percent_signed`
  using `u128` widening so `100 * num * 10^precision` arithmetic
  doesn't overflow native widths. Rounding is round-half-up via
  `+ denom/2`. Precision is internally capped at 38 to keep
  `10^precision` inside `u128::MAX`.
- abi: thin shims that delegate to core, then copy into the
  bounded buffer with snprintf-style truncation.

Tests: 11 core unit tests covering integer percentages, fractional
rounding (1/3 → 33.33, 2/3 → 66.67), zero-denom defensive output,
signed-zero sign suppression, percent-at-or-above-100,
pathological-precision saturation, and a 10-digit reference value
for 1/7. 12 abi integration tests covering all wrapper paths
including bounded-buffer truncation, NULL-buf / zero-bufsize, and
all four sign permutations for strspct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
